content:
As far as I can see ffi:c_size_t is still an unstable feature.
I've seen the very awkward behavior where two C-functions (memcpy, devres_alloc) take a size_t, but if you pass a Rust size, _one_ of the two functions is satisfied, whereas the other complains about expecting a u64.
If you change to u64, it's the other way around
The xarray patchset uses some logic to convert between them asserting that its lossless.

convert between whom?

My issue is that it seems that the binding mechanism treats the same size_t differently.
In one scenario, it wants to mapp it to a Rust u64, in the other to a Rust usize
Note that kernel size_t is defined as unsigned int or unsigned long depending on arch and is not the C size_t.
I think that might be a breaking change to userspace, since now __kernel_size_t is different for 32-bit archs.

Hm, that's unfortunate..
Then something must be fishy on the C side I guess; some compilation units seem to pull in a different size_t definition on the same architecture
